name: Fruit Crescents
recipeInstructions:
You can use any type of fruit preserves, such as raspberry.
These crescents are easy to make. First, in a large bowl, mix together the softened cream cheese and butter with the flour until you have a smooth dough ball. Chill the dough for about 30 minutes.
Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Roll out the chilled dough on wax paper, forming it into thin rectangles about 1/8 to 1/4 inch thick. Cut each rectangle into 3-inch squares.
Place a small amount of preserves near the center of each square. Fold the dough over to enclose the preserves and shape them into triangles, leaving a slight gap at the top where the folded edge doesn't quite meet the bottom.
Roll the dough toward the pointed end, smoothing and sealing the edges as you go. Turn the ends inward to create a crescent shape.
Bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until lightly browned. Remove from the oven and let cool on wire racks before rolling each piece in confectioners sugar.
Yield: Approximately 2 dozen crescents.
